title: Chef and Minimum Cost of His Tree

content.problemStatement:
You are a given a tree of NNN vertices. The iii-th vertex has two things assigned to it — a value AiA_iAi​ and a range [Li,Ri][L_i, R_i][Li​,Ri​]. It is guaranteed that Li≤Ai≤RiL_i \leq A_i \leq R_iLi​≤Ai​≤Ri​.

You perform the following operation **exactly once**:


Pick a (possibly empty) subset SSS of vertices of the tree. SSS must satisfy the additional condition that no two vertices in SSS are directly connected by an edge, i.e, it is an independent set.
Then, for each x∈Sx \in Sx∈S, you can set AxA_xAx​ to any value in the range [Lx,Rx][L_x, R_x][Lx​,Rx​] (endpoints included).
Your task is to minimize the value of ∑∣Au−Av∣\sum |A_u - A_v|∑∣Au​−Av​∣ over all **unordered** pairs (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) such that uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of vertices.
The next NNN lines describe the values of the vertices. The iii-th of these NNN lines contains three space-separated integers LiL_iLi​, AiA_iAi​, and RiR_iRi​ respectively
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible value as described in the statement.
constraints: ui≠viu_i \neq v_iui​=vi​ for each 1≤i≤N−11 \leq i \leq N-11≤i≤N−1
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
